name: Stribog
description: Stribog is a Slavic deity associated with winds, sky, and air. He is considered a powerful and often unpredictable god, capable of both gentle breezes and destructive storms. His origins are somewhat obscure, but he is believed to be one of the older gods in the Slavic pantheon. Stribog is often depicted as an old man with a horn or a trumpet, which he uses to control the winds. He can summon storms, direct the wind's path, and even ride on the wind itself. While not inherently malevolent, Stribog's power makes him a force to be reckoned with, and his actions can have significant consequences for mortals. He is often invoked by sailors and farmers seeking favorable winds or protection from storms. Some interpretations link him to the dispersal of seeds and the bringing of new life, connecting him to agricultural cycles. He is sometimes considered a grandfather or elder figure to other wind deities or spirits, further emphasizing his authority over the air. His influence extends beyond simple weather control, encompassing the very breath of life and the movement of energy through the world. Stribog's role highlights the Slavs' deep connection to the natural world and their respect for its powerful forces. He is a reminder that even the most seemingly invisible elements can hold immense power and influence over human lives.
subtitle: God of Winds, Sky, and Air

keyMyths.title: Stribog's Fury
keyMyths.description: Stribog unleashes a powerful storm to punish those who disrespect the natural world. The storm serves as a reminder of his power and the importance of respecting the elements.

keyMyths.title: Guidance of the Winds
keyMyths.description: Sailors pray to Stribog for favorable winds to guide their ships across the sea. He grants their request, ensuring a safe and prosperous journey.

keyMyths.title: Whispers of Stribog
keyMyths.description: Farmers listen to the whispers of Stribog in the wind to determine the best time for planting crops. His guidance ensures a bountiful harvest.

sources.author: Helmold of Bosau
sources.description: A medieval chronicle providing early accounts of Slavic beliefs.
sources.text: A medieval chronicle providing early accounts of Slavic beliefs.

sources.author: Gieysztor, Aleksander
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Slavic mythology.
